text: When a customer's architectural design is flawed, you can push back gracefully by saying: "While your setup is functional, it {{c1::introduces a single point of failure}} that could {{c2::compromise high availability}} during peak traffic."
explanation: Pointing out design risks (like single points of failure) using objective technical concepts rather than telling the client they are 'wrong' avoids defensiveness and builds advisory trust.
usage:
Pattern: [Setup] introduces [risk] that could compromise [performance metric]
  • This setup introduces a single point of failure that could compromise high availability.
  • Using a single DB instance introduces a bottleneck that could compromise throughput.
spanish: Cuando el diseño arquitectónico de un cliente es defectuoso, puedes retroceder con gracia diciendo: "Aunque su configuración es funcional, introduce un punto único de falla que podría comprometer la alta disponibilidad durante los picos de tráfico".

text: To handle a client demanding a new capability as a bug fix, say: "This behavior {{c1::aligns with current system design}}; however, I will {{c2::advocate for your use case}} directly with our product team."
explanation: "Aligns with current system design" is a professional way to state that the system is not broken, while "advocate for your use case" shows you are still partner-focused.
usage:
Pattern: [Behavior] aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for [use case]
  • The login timeout aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for your use case of extending it.
  • Not allowing bulk deletions aligns with current system design.
spanish: Para manejar a un cliente que exige una nueva capacidad como si fuera una corrección de errores, di: "Este comportamiento se alinea con el diseño actual del sistema; sin embargo, defenderé su caso de uso directamente con nuestro equipo de producto".

text: In a Post-Mortem or Root Cause Analysis (RCA), always close by saying: "To prevent recurrence, we are {{c1::implementing automated safeguards}} and {{c2::refining our circuit breaker thresholds}}."
explanation: An RCA must culminate in concrete, systemic preventive actions. Explaining the safeguards demonstrates that the organization has learned and adapted to protect the client's operations.
usage:
Pattern: To prevent recurrence, we are implementing [preventative action]
  • To prevent recurrence, we are implementing automated safeguards and database limits.
  • We are refining our circuit breaker thresholds to prevent cascade failures.
spanish: En un análisis de causa raíz (RCA), siempre cierra diciendo: "Para evitar que vuelva a ocurrir, estamos implementando salvaguardas automatizadas y refinando los límites de nuestros interruptores automáticos".

text: To politely refuse debugging custom scripts, say: "While I can confirm our API is responding normally, {{c1::custom scripts are outside our support scope}}; however, you can reference this {{c2::sample payload}}."
explanation: Clearly states the platform's boundaries (outside our support scope) but remains helpful by providing a reference example to guide the customer.
usage:
Pattern: [Topic] is outside our support scope; however, you can reference [alternative resource]
  • Debugging custom scripts is outside our support scope; however, you can reference our sample repository.
  • Third-party libraries are outside our support scope.
spanish: Para rechazar cortésmente la depuración de scripts personalizados, di: "Aunque puedo confirmar que nuestra API está respondiendo normalmente, los scripts personalizados están fuera de nuestro alcance de soporte; sin embargo, puede consultar este ejemplo de carga útil".

text: Instead of apologizing for a delay, use appreciation to stay assertive: "Thank you for your patience while we {{c1::conducted a deep-dive review}} into your {{c2::infrastructure configuration}}."
explanation: Saying 'thank you for your patience' validates the wait while asserting that the time was spent on a high-value action (deep-dive review), preserving authority.
usage:
Pattern: Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into [infrastructure/database/setup]
  • Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into your replication logs.
  • Our team conducted a deep-dive review and resolved the memory leak.
spanish: En lugar de disculparse por un retraso, use el agradecimiento para mantener la asertividad: "Gracias por su paciencia mientras realizábamos una revisión profunda de la configuración de su infraestructura".

text: To deflect a premature escalation to management, suggest: "I want to ensure we {{c1::exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths}} before we {{c2::engage our engineering lead}}, so we can present them with complete data."
explanation: This phrase positions the delay in escalation as a benefit to the customer (ensuring they have complete data first), maintaining control of the troubleshooting flow.
usage:
Pattern: exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage [lead/expert]
  • I want to ensure we exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage our engineering lead.
  • Let's exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before escalation.
spanish: Para desviar una escalación prematura a la gerencia, sugiera: "Quiero asegurarme de que agotemos todas las vías de diagnóstico inmediato antes de involucrar a nuestro líder de ingeniería, para que podamos presentarles datos completos".

text: To admit an error without losing credibility, state: "Our initial assessment {{c1::missed X variable}}; we have {{c2::adjusted our troubleshooting path}} to focus on Y to get you back online safely."
explanation: Directly acknowledges the new information (missed variable) and immediately pivots to the updated action plan, projecting competence and responsiveness rather than defensiveness.
usage:
Pattern: Our initial assessment missed [variable]; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on [focus]
  • Our initial assessment missed the firewall block; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on security rules.
  • We adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on replication delays.
spanish: Para admitir un error sin perder credibilidad, declare: "Nuestra evaluación inicial pasó por alto la variable X; hemos ajustado nuestro camino de resolución de problemas para enfocarnos en Y para que vuelva a estar en línea de manera segura".

text: To pivot a call when time is running out, say: "We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we {{c1::lock down next steps}} for your production deployment, let's {{c2::pivot to the action items}}."
explanation: Using "lock down next steps" and "pivot to action items" focuses the remaining time on outcomes, preventing the meeting from ending without clear deliverables.
usage:
Pattern: We have [time] left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for [goal], let's pivot to [action items]
  • We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for your production migration, let's pivot to the action items.
  • Let's make sure we lock down next steps before the meeting ends.
spanish: Para reorientar una llamada cuando se acaba el tiempo, di: "Nos quedan 15 minutos en nuestro bloque programado. Para asegurarnos de acordar los próximos pasos para su implementación en producción, pasemos a los elementos de acción".

text: To politely interrupt a client repeating the same complaint: "I hear you, and to ensure we don't {{c1::lose ground on the diagnostic work}}, let's focus on collecting the server logs."
explanation: "Lose ground on" means to lose progress already made. This redirect politely signals that repeating the complaint stops active troubleshooting.
usage:
Pattern: I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on [work/diagnostics], let's focus on [next step]
  • I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on the diagnostic work, let's focus on collecting the server logs.
  • We don't want to lose ground on the replication analysis.
spanish: Para interrumpir cortésmente a un cliente que repite la misma queja: "Lo escucho, y para asegurarnos de no perder terreno en el trabajo de diagnóstico, concentrémonos en recopilar los registros del servidor".

text: We need to conduct a Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to determine why the database crashed during the {{c1::peak traffic hour}}.
explanation: 'Peak traffic hour' refers to the specific time of day when a web service or network receives the highest volume of users or transactions.
usage:
Pattern: peak traffic / high load times
  • Our infrastructure must scale up automatically during peak traffic.
  • Latency spikes occur mostly during peak traffic hours.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar un Análisis de Causa Raíz (RCA) para determinar por qué la base de datos colapsó durante la hora de mayor tráfico.

text: We need to conduct a post-mortem analysis to determine the root cause of the memory leak in our production {{c1::servers}}.
explanation: A 'production server' (or prod server) is the live server hosting the application active for real users, distinct from staging or test servers.
usage:
Pattern: production servers / deploy to production
  • Do not run untested scripts directly on production servers.
  • The production servers crashed due to high CPU load.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar un análisis posterior al incidente (post-mortem) para determinar la causa raíz de la fuga de memoria en nuestros servidores de producción.

text: Our immediate focus is to apply a hotfix to restore service, then work on a long-term {{c1::architectural fix}}.
explanation: An 'architectural fix' is a fundamental redesign of database schemas, code modules, or network topologies to resolve a recurring issue permanently.
usage:
Pattern: architectural fix / permanent redesign
  • Applying a temporary workaround is not an alternative to an architectural fix.
  • The architect proposed a database partitioning model as the architectural fix.
spanish: Nuestro enfoque inmediato es aplicar un parche para restablecer el servicio y luego trabajar en una corrección arquitectónica a largo plazo.

text: The crash was caused by a database deadlock; we need to optimize our queries and transaction isolation {{c1::levels}}.
explanation: 'Transaction isolation levels' in SQL databases control the visibility of data to other concurrent transactions, preventing data corruption.
usage:
Pattern: isolation levels / read committed / serializable
  • Setting high transaction isolation levels can increase locks and cause deadlocks.
  • We set the database isolation level to Read Committed.
spanish: El colapso fue causado por un bloqueo mutuo (deadlock) de la base de datos; necesitamos optimizar nuestras consultas y niveles de aislamiento de transacciones.

text: Let's review the API response times to identify when the performance degradation began to {{c1::affect users}}.
explanation: To 'affect users' means that software bugs, latency, or outages begin to disrupt the client experience or cause errors on user devices.
usage:
Pattern: affect users / impact clients
  • The database outage did not affect users because of the fallback cache.
  • How many clients did the latency spike affect?
spanish: Revisemos los tiempos de respuesta de la API para identificar cuándo la degradación del rendimiento comenzó a afectar a los usuarios.

text: We need to negotiate the SLA details: what is the penalty if the uptime drops below the guaranteed {{c1::99.9%}}?
explanation: '99.9%' (three nines) is a standard service level agreement uptime metric, permitting no more than 8.76 hours of downtime per year.
usage:
Pattern: 99.9% uptime / 99.99% availability
  • The hosting provider committed to a 99.9% uptime guarantee.
  • Our service fell below the 99.9% threshold this month.
spanish: Necesitamos negociar los detalles del SLA: ¿cuál es la penalización si el tiempo de actividad cae por debajo del 99.9% garantizado?

text: The post-mortem report indicates that the outage could have been avoided if we had set up server resource monitoring {{c1::alerts}}.
explanation: A 'monitoring alert' is an automated notification (sent via Slack, PagerDuty, or email) triggered when system metrics (like CPU or memory) cross a threshold.
usage:
Pattern: monitoring alerts / trigger an alert
  • Configure monitoring alerts to notify the team when disk space is low.
  • The database memory leak triggered several monitoring alerts.
spanish: El informe posterior al incidente indica que la interrupción podría haberse evitado si hubiéramos configurado alertas de monitoreo de recursos del servidor.

text: Let's establish a roll-back plan before we deploy this update to production to minimize potential service {{c1::disruptions}}.
explanation: 'Service disruptions' are events where software systems or cloud networks become unavailable, causing operations to halt.
usage:
Pattern: service disruptions / business disruption
  • The network migration caused minimal service disruptions.
  • Avoid service disruptions by testing all updates in staging first.
spanish: Establezcamos un plan de reversión antes de implementar esta actualización en producción para minimizar posibles interrupciones del servicio.

text: We need to run load tests to verify that the API gateway can handle up to double our normal request {{c1::volume}}.
explanation: 'Request volume' refers to the total number of HTTP requests processed by a web server or gateway within a specific timeframe.
usage:
Pattern: request volume / traffic volume / concurrent requests
  • Our API gateway handles a high request volume during marketing events.
  • The server crashed because the request volume exceeded its limits.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar pruebas de carga para verificar que la pasarela API pueda manejar hasta el doble de nuestro volumen de solicitudes normal.

text: The root cause was a configuration mismatch between the staging and production environment {{c1::variables}}.
explanation: 'Environment variables' are dynamic-valued objects stored in the operating system that define configuration values (like DB paths, keys) for running processes.
usage:
Pattern: environment variables / config variables
  • Make sure you do not commit environment variables containing secrets.
  • The app crashed because the database URL environment variable was missing.
spanish: La causa raíz fue una discrepancia de configuración entre las variables de entorno de los entornos de prueba (staging) y producción.

text: We will document our findings and remediation steps in a formal RCA report and share it with the client {{c1::tomorrow}}.
explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, set here as the target deadline to share the Root Cause Analysis report with stakeholders.
usage:
Pattern: share it tomorrow / report tomorrow
  • The slides are ready; we will share the report tomorrow.
  • I will email you the updates tomorrow.
spanish: Documentaremos nuestros hallazgos y pasos de remediación en un informe formal de RCA y lo compartiremos con el cliente mañana.
